methodsAttempted.query: site:murdoch.edu.au Murdoch University generative AI policy students assessment academic integrity ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search mostly surfaced Murdoch Research Portal PDFs and no central public AI policy page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:libguides.murdoch.edu.au/genAI Generative AI tools for study and research Murdoch
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl found the Murdoch University Library public guide with pages for generative AI use, assessment of tools, tool listings, and further support.
Verified www.murdoch.edu.au and libguides.murdoch.edu.au as official Murdoch University web properties. SharePoint and Moodle links were documented as support targets but not fetched behind login.

methodsAttempted.query: site:murdoch.edu.au "Artificial Intelligence at Murdoch" "Guiding Principles for Generative AI"
methodsAttempted.notes: Found a broad central AI-at-Murdoch page and references to SharePoint-hosted guiding principles. The public central page was too general for policy claims; the SharePoint page was treated as inaccessible for public crawl evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a public Murdoch University Library guide with substantive guidance on generative AI use, tool assessment, Copilot access, copyright, academic-integrity risk, and support pathways. No public central binding AI policy was found; claims are limited to the Library guidance and do not treat inaccessible SharePoint guiding principles as direct evidence.

Approved as a Library tool-treatment claim, not a university-wide procurement approval statement.
Approved because the Copilot section directly describes Murdoch's Microsoft licensing agreement and protected sign-in indicator.

artifacts.title: Murdoch University generative AI guidance source review
artifacts.summary: Public discovery did not find a crawlable central binding Murdoch University AI policy. The strongest public official evidence is a Murdoch University Library guide on generative AI tools for study and research. Approved staged claims are therefore scoped to Library guidance on confirming allowed AI use, academic-misconduct risk for unauthorized AI use, copyright and Murdoch IP restrictions, Library tool assessment criteria, and Copilot enterprise-protection guidance. SharePoint guiding principles and Moodle AI toolkit links were documented but not crawled or used as direct evidence because they appear to require authenticated access.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No public central binding AI policy was found during this run; the SharePoint guiding-principles page was linked from a public Library page but not accessible as public crawl evidence.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not marked canonical or human reviewed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
